content:
Short title and application
1. (1) This Act may be cited as the Tolls (Roads and Bridges) Act
1965.
(2) This Act shall apply to the States of Peninsular Malaysia
only.
Imposition of tolls

Imposition of tolls
2. (1) It shall be lawful for the Minister charged with responsibility
for works with the concurrence of the Treasury, from time to time
by order to impose tolls to be paid for the use by vehicles of such
roads or any part thereof or of such bridges as may be specified
in the order.
In this section, the expressions “roads” and “bridges” mean
federal roads and federal bridges, respectively.
(2) Any order made under subsection (1) shall specify—
(a) the vehicles or any class thereof in respect of which tolls
are payable;
6
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 416
(b) the amounts of such tolls;
(c) the place where such tolls are to be collected; and
(d) the person to whom such tolls are to be paid.
Barrier may be erected
3.
The Minister may cause a barrier to be erected across the road
at any place where tolls are to be paid, and such barrier may be
kept closed against any vehicle until the toll due in respect of such
vehicle has been paid.
Penalty for contravention of any order
4.
The driver of any vehicle contravening any order made under
section 2 shall be guilty of an offence and shall on conviction be
liable to a fine not exceeding fifty ringgit.
Exemption
5.
The Minister may by order exempt any vehicle from the payment
of tolls.
7
Tolls (Roads and Bridges)
